summary: Backend refactor regression testing complete. server.py was split into 16 route modules + shared.py. All 28 endpoints from the review request return correct responses with expected data shapes, and RBAC / auth guards are preserved post-refactor. 34/34 pytest tests passed (100%).

backend: TestAuth::test_login_returns_session_token - POST /api/auth/login returns session_token + user (sets httpOnly cookie)
TestContact::test_create_lead_no_auth - POST /api/contact/lead creates lead without auth -> 200/201

critical_code_review_comments: Auth uses httpOnly cookie `session_token` in addition to Bearer header - this is fine but test harnesses must use fresh sessions when asserting 401 behaviour (documented in test file via _fresh() helper).
shared.py (586 lines) is large but still under the 700-line soft threshold; consider further splitting into shared/db.py, shared/auth.py, shared/constants.py for maintainability.
server.py @app.on_event('startup'/'shutdown') uses deprecated FastAPI event handlers; migrate to lifespan context manager on a future pass (non-blocking).
CORS fallback sets allow_origins=['*'] while allow_credentials=True - browsers reject this combination. Production CORS_ORIGINS is set, but the fallback branch is unsafe; log warning already present.
updated_files: /app/backend/tests/test_refactor_endpoints.py (new pytest regression suite for refactored endpoints)
seed_data_creation: POST /api/contact/lead created a TEST_ prefixed lead (TEST_refactor_lead@example.com).
context_for_next_testing_agent: Refactor of monolithic server.py into 16 route modules + shared.py is verified. All 28 endpoints from the review request + 6 auth/RBAC guards = 34 tests pass. Regression file is /app/backend/tests/test_refactor_endpoints.py. Note: /api/auth/login sets a session_token httpOnly cookie, so if you reuse a requests.Session you remain authenticated even without Authorization header - use _fresh() helper for unauthenticated assertions.
